title: odontites serotinus subsp. calcicola
intro: Odontites serotinus subsp. calcicola, also known as a subspecies of the Odontites serotinus plant, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, and is often found in limestone areas with grasslands and rocky outcrops. The plant has been reported to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin conditions. Odontites serotinus subsp. calcicola can be used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
